description_long: This is a whole head of Savoy cabbage, characterized by its large, rounded form and deeply crinkled, textured leaves. The outer leaves are a pale to medium green, with prominent veins and a slightly waxy appearance. The leaves are tightly layered, forming a dense, compact head with a rosette-like structure. The surface is uneven and bumpy due to the pronounced leaf veins and undulating leaf margins. The base of the cabbage is slightly flattened, and the central stem is not clearly visible from any angle. The overall appearance is fresh and robust, typical of a cabbage found in markets or urban gardens.
description: A dense, rounded head of Savoy cabbage with crinkled, pale green leaves and a textured, veined surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the cabbage from a slightly top-side angle, highlighting the overlapping, crinkled leaves and the prominent central vein.
description_view_1: This view presents the cabbage more from the side, emphasizing the rounded shape and the dense layering of the leaves.
description_view_2: This angle displays the cabbage's side and top, showing the undulating leaf margins and the compactness of the head.
description_view_3: This view shows the cabbage from the base side, with the bottom leaves and the slightly flattened base visible.
